verses.text: In His own country they are astonished, 'Is not this the carpenter?' and are offended. Familiarity blinds. They despise the Saviour they think they know.
In His own country they are astonished, 'Is not this the carpenter?' and are offended. Familiarity blinds. They despise the Saviour they think they know.
In His own country they are astonished, 'Is not this the carpenter?' and are offended. Familiarity blinds. They despise the Saviour they think they know.
'A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country'; He could do no mighty work for their unbelief. Unbelief binds. It shuts the door on Christ's power.
'A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country'; He could do no mighty work for their unbelief. Unbelief binds. It shuts the door on Christ's power.
'A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country'; He could do no mighty work for their unbelief. Unbelief binds. It shuts the door on Christ's power.
He sends the twelve two by two, with power over unclean spirits, taking nothing. Sent in trust. They go light, leaning on God's provision.
He sends the twelve two by two, with power over unclean spirits, taking nothing. Sent in trust. They go light, leaning on God's provision.
He sends the twelve two by two, with power over unclean spirits, taking nothing. Sent in trust. They go light, leaning on God's provision.
Abide where ye are received; shake off the dust where refused; they preach and heal. Bold mission. They proclaim repentance and cast out devils.
Abide where ye are received; shake off the dust where refused; they preach and heal. Bold mission. They proclaim repentance and cast out devils.
Abide where ye are received; shake off the dust where refused; they preach and heal. Bold mission. They proclaim repentance and cast out devils.

Herod, hearing, fears it is John, whom he beheaded, risen again. Guilty conscience. The sin he buried rises to haunt him.
Herod, hearing, fears it is John, whom he beheaded, risen again. Guilty conscience. The sin he buried rises to haunt him.
Herod, hearing, fears it is John, whom he beheaded, risen again. Guilty conscience. The sin he buried rises to haunt him.
He had bound John for Herodias's sake, yet feared and 'heard him gladly.' Divided heart. He loved the preacher yet kept the sin.
He had bound John for Herodias's sake, yet feared and 'heard him gladly.' Divided heart. He loved the preacher yet kept the sin.
He had bound John for Herodias's sake, yet feared and 'heard him gladly.' Divided heart. He loved the preacher yet kept the sin.
He had bound John for Herodias's sake, yet feared and 'heard him gladly.' Divided heart. He loved the preacher yet kept the sin.
At his birthday the dancing daughter, prompted, asks for John's head. Rash oath. A foolish vow becomes the snare for murder.
At his birthday the dancing daughter, prompted, asks for John's head. Rash oath. A foolish vow becomes the snare for murder.
At his birthday the dancing daughter, prompted, asks for John's head. Rash oath. A foolish vow becomes the snare for murder.
At his birthday the dancing daughter, prompted, asks for John's head. Rash oath. A foolish vow becomes the snare for murder.
At his birthday the dancing daughter, prompted, asks for John's head. Rash oath. A foolish vow becomes the snare for murder.
Sorry but bound by his oath, he beheads John; his disciples bury him. Cowardly pride. He kills a holy man rather than break a wicked word.
Sorry but bound by his oath, he beheads John; his disciples bury him. Cowardly pride. He kills a holy man rather than break a wicked word.
Sorry but bound by his oath, he beheads John; his disciples bury him. Cowardly pride. He kills a holy man rather than break a wicked word.
Sorry but bound by his oath, he beheads John; his disciples bury him. Cowardly pride. He kills a holy man rather than break a wicked word.
The apostles return; 'come... apart... and rest a while,' for they had no leisure. Needful rest. Christ cares for the bodies of His weary servants.
The apostles return; 'come... apart... and rest a while,' for they had no leisure. Needful rest. Christ cares for the bodies of His weary servants.
The apostles return; 'come... apart... and rest a while,' for they had no leisure. Needful rest. Christ cares for the bodies of His weary servants.
The crowds run ahead; moved with compassion 'as sheep not having a shepherd,' He teaches them. Shepherd's pity. He feeds the soul before the body.
The crowds run ahead; moved with compassion 'as sheep not having a shepherd,' He teaches them. Shepherd's pity. He feeds the soul before the body.

'Give ye them to eat'; with five loaves and two fishes He sits them in ranks. Small store, great order. He prepares to feed a multitude from almost nothing.
'Give ye them to eat'; with five loaves and two fishes He sits them in ranks. Small store, great order. He prepares to feed a multitude from almost nothing.
'Give ye them to eat'; with five loaves and two fishes He sits them in ranks. Small store, great order. He prepares to feed a multitude from almost nothing.
'Give ye them to eat'; with five loaves and two fishes He sits them in ranks. Small store, great order. He prepares to feed a multitude from almost nothing.
He blesses and breaks, and all are filled, twelve baskets over; five thousand men. Bountiful supply. In His hands the little is more than enough.

He sends them by ship and goes to pray; seeing them toiling, He comes walking on the sea. Watchful Lord. He sees His struggling own and comes to them.
He sends them by ship and goes to pray; seeing them toiling, He comes walking on the sea. Watchful Lord. He sees His struggling own and comes to them.
He sends them by ship and goes to pray; seeing them toiling, He comes walking on the sea. Watchful Lord. He sees His struggling own and comes to them.
He sends them by ship and goes to pray; seeing them toiling, He comes walking on the sea. Watchful Lord. He sees His struggling own and comes to them.
Fearing a spirit, they hear, 'Be of good cheer; it is I'; the wind ceases, and they wonder. Hardened hearts. Even after the loaves, they are slow to believe.

At Gennesaret the sick touch His garment's hem and are made whole. Healing virtue. Wherever He goes, mercy flows to the needy.
